notes: Improving indoor air quality after water damage in Australian settings. Real taught tools only — moisture meters, infrared thermal imaging cameras, air sampling, HEPA air scrubbers, dehumidifiers, air movers. Technicians in workwear/gloves, metric, 230 V / AU equipment. Bright, clean, professional. Keep clinical — no dramatic mould.

images.context: Course hero image for 'Improving Indoor Air Quality After Water Damage'
images.prompt: A professional restoration technician in an Australian home assessing indoor air quality after water damage, holding an infrared thermal imaging camera toward a wall while a HEPA air scrubber and a portable dehumidifier run in the background drying the room, bright clean daylight, gloves and workwear. Realistic documentary photography, mid-distance over-shoulder framing.
images.negativePrompt: no on-image text, no logos, no brand names, no watermarks, no gore, no distressing/dramatic mould close-ups, no identifiable face-forward portraits, no readable text or numbers on device screens, no visible mould growth, no standing water, no US power outlets

images.context: Module on tools for assessing air quality — moisture meters and infrared thermography
images.prompt: Close mid-distance of a technician's gloved hands using a pin-type moisture meter on a skirting board while holding an infrared thermal imaging camera that shows a cool moisture patch on the wall, detecting hidden moisture in a bright Australian room. Realistic photography, over-shoulder framing on the tools.
